existing_Entity_Description: Merritt is a municipality in Thompson-Nicola & Cariboo, British Columbia, Canada, within the Thompson-Nicola. As of the 2021 Canadian Census, Merritt had a population of 7,051. The community is situated in BC's rugged interior — ranching country, cowboy culture, and wide-open landscape. a resource-based economy with steady local service demand. The region features hot, arid summers and cold winters with dramatic seasonal shifts.

REGION_BUNDLE.climate: Dry interior continental climate: hot summers (Kamloops and Lytton record some of Canada's highest temperatures), cold winters, low precipitation; grassland wildfire risk and freeze-thaw cycles define exterior maintenance.
REGION_BUNDLE.terrain: Sagebrush grasslands and hoodoos where the North and South Thompson rivers meet at Kamloops, rising to the Cariboo Plateau's ranchland and lakes country further north.
REGION_BUNDLE.economy: Ranching country (the historic Cariboo Gold Rush trail), mining (Highland Valley Copper near Logan Lake is Canada's largest copper mine), forestry, and transport — Kamloops sits at the junction of the Trans-Canada and Yellowhead highways with Thompson Rivers University.
REGION_BUNDLE.building_stock: Ranch houses and acreages, post-war grid neighbourhoods in Kamloops and Williams Lake, lakefront cabins, and manufactured homes in rural areas.
